role: Son of Ayatollah Khomeini; political liaison; died 1995 under suspicious circumstances
bio: Ahmad Khomeini was the son of [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) and served as the key liaison between the founder of the Islamic Republic and the political establishment. After his father's death in 1989, he remained an influential figure who maintained access to Khomeini's archives and political legacy. On 17 March 1995, Ahmad Khomeini died of an apparent heart attack at the age of 49. The death is widely believed to have been an assassination — reportedly by cyanide poisoning carried out by Saeed Emami — as part of the broader [chain murders](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998) campaign. Ahmad had reportedly been collecting documents that could have been embarrassing to the hardline establishment, and his death conveniently removed a potential rival centre of revolutionary legitimacy.
editorial_notes: Born 1945 Qom. Son of Ayatollah Khomeini. Died 17 March 1995 — officially heart attack, widely believed poisoned with cyanide by Saeed Emami. Unconfirmed.
